content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer **X** such that `0 ≤ X < 100`.
Your task is to find the difference between **100** and **X**, i.e., compute **(100 - X)**.


Input Format

A single integer **X** — the given number.


Output Format

Output a single integer — the value of **100 - X**.
content.outputFormat: Print a single integer on a new line.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we simply need to subtract the given integer X from 100. This will give us the difference between 100 and X.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) because we are performing a constant time operation (subtraction).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) because we are not using any additional space that scales with input size.
